programs.name: LENS @ JHU — Learning Engineering for Next-Generation Systems
programs.summary: Concentration within JHU MEd in Learning Design & Technology. Targets practitioners in complex organizations: defense, healthcare, large-scale education. Grounded in human systems integration and learning engineering expertise. Capstone produces evidence dashboard, reproducible report, and governance/ethics plan. Unique JHU ecosystem: APL + Medicine + IEEE/ICICLE partnership.

programs.name: Human-Computer Interaction Institute — CMU
programs.summary: Home of Ken Koedinger and Cognitive Tutor / LearnLab. One of the premier academic homes for LE research globally. PhD and MS programs.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Virtual Institute (LEVI)
programs.summary: Funds multi-year cohorts building AI-driven, research-backed tools to improve outcomes in math (LEVI Math) and early literacy (LEVI Literacy), with rapid experimentation and rigorous evaluation. Not affiliated with the earlier iNACOL/Aurora convening sometimes also called LEVI.

programs.name: Open Learning Initiative (OLI)
programs.summary: Flagship CMU learning-engineering R&D: course development, instructional training, consulting, and platforms (including Torus) for evidence-based, continuously improving digital learning tied to the Simon Initiative.

programs.name: Ken Koedinger
programs.summary: Co-originator of learning engineering as a field. Creator of Cognitive Tutor. Pioneer of PSLC DataShop and LearnLab. Foundational theorist and practitioner.

programs.name: Ryan Baker
programs.summary: Pioneer in educational data mining and learning analytics; directs the Penn Center for Learning Analytics. Author of key EDM methods handbook. EDM journal editor. Research spans learning engineering, intelligent tutors, and quantitative ethnography.

programs.name: Bror Saxberg
programs.summary: Early champion of learning engineering as a named field. Wrote influential 2016 Atlantic piece calling for learning engineers. Practitioner-bridge figure.

programs.name: Jim Goodell
programs.summary: Co-founder of Learning Engineering book (IEEE Press). Co-editor of canonical LE textbook. Core IEEE ICICLE contributor.

programs.name: John Behrens
programs.summary: Evidence-centered design; learning analytics at scale; psychometrics meets LE. Key bridge between assessment science and learning engineering.

programs.name: Learning @ Scale
programs.summary: Premier venue for LE research at scale. Emphasizes empirical evidence and adaptive/personalized learning. Annual.

programs.name: Educational Data Mining (EDM)
programs.summary: Annual international conference and journal. Primary venue for learning analytics and EDM research.

programs.name: Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED)
programs.summary: Flagship AI in education conference. ITS sub-conference often co-located. Annual; alternating with ITS.

programs.name: Learning Analytics & Knowledge (LAK)
programs.summary: Premier analytics in education conference. Strong on data infrastructure and equity.

programs.name: IEEE ICICLE Annual Meeting
programs.summary: Annual community gathering for the IEEE learning engineering community. Standards, BoK, and credentialing discussions.

programs.name: ASSISTments
programs.summary: Free ITS platform widely used for math tutoring research. Large research base. RCTs conducted on platform.

programs.name: DataShop / LearnSphere
programs.summary: Largest open repository of learning interaction data. Enables corpus expansion and field-level analysis.

programs.name: xAPI / Tin Can API
programs.summary: Standard for learning activity data. Foundation for interoperable learning record stores. Key infrastructure standard.

programs.name: IEEE ICICLE
programs.summary: International Community for IEEE Learning Engineering. Primary professional home for LE. Developing BoK, standards, credentialing. Resources page is a primary seed source for this corpus.

programs.name: Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR)
programs.summary: International nonprofit dedicated to research, practice, and policy of learning analytics. Home of LAK conference and companion journal.

programs.name: International Society for the Learning Sciences (ISLS)
programs.summary: Home of CSCL and ICLS conferences. Bridges learning science and design. Important for T01 foundation layer.

programs.name: Learning Engineering (IEEE Press textbook)
programs.summary: The canonical textbook of the field. Two-part structure. End notes are a primary seed source for citation expansion. IEEE Press, 2022.

programs.name: IEEE ICICLE Resources Page
programs.summary: Curated resource list maintained by IEEE ICICLE community. Includes papers, tools, programs, and community resources. Lacks cohesion and learning journey structure — this corpus addresses that gap.

programs.name: How People Learn I & II
programs.summary: Foundational synthesis of learning science for educators. Free online. Widely cited. Essential T01 anchor.

programs.name: Saxberg 2016 Atlantic: 'Forgotten Role of Learning Engineer'
programs.summary: Canonical entry point essay calling for the learning engineering profession. Widely cited. Short. Often assigned reading.

programs.name: ICICLE Body of Knowledge (BoK) — current draft
programs.summary: The field's emerging canonical definition document. Defines competency areas, vocabulary, and practice standards.

programs.name: Learning Engineering for Online Education (Dede, Richards, Saxberg eds.)
programs.summary: Edited volume by Dede, Richards, and Saxberg — the primary book-length treatment of learning engineering applied to online education. Covers LE process, adaptive systems, evidence, and scale.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Process Description & Diagram (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Official ICICLE description of the Learning Engineering process, including the iterative cycle diagram showing problem framing, design, measurement, and improvement steps.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Case Guide 1.0 (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Slides deck walking through real LE case examples. Shows how the LE process is applied in practice: problem formulation, data collection, evidence-based iteration.

programs.name: Generalizable Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model
programs.summary: PDF defining a maturity model for organizations adopting LE practices. Stages describe progression from ad hoc instructional design through fully evidence-based, data-driven learning improvement.

programs.name: Five Whys Root-Cause Analysis Template (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc template for applying the Five Whys technique to identify root causes of learning performance gaps. Designed for the LE needs-analysis phase.

programs.name: Fishbone / Cause-Effect Analysis Template (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc template for Ishikawa (fishbone) cause-and-effect diagrams in learning contexts. Used during LE problem framing and needs analysis to map contributing factors to learning gaps.

programs.name: Performance Task Analysis Template (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc template for structured task decomposition in learning engineering. Guides practitioners through breaking complex performance into observable subtasks to inform instructional design.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Evidence Decision Tracker (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Drive folder with templates for tracking design decisions alongside the evidence that motivated them throughout the LE process. Supports reproducible, evidence-based practice.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Implementation Checklist (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc checklist for practitioners implementing a learning engineering project. Covers project phases from needs analysis through evaluation and dissemination.

programs.name: Examples of Roles & Expertise on an LE Team (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc describing the multidisciplinary roles typically needed on a learning engineering team: learning scientists, data analysts, instructional designers, engineers, subject matter experts.

programs.name: Learning Design Principles Toolkit (McEldoon, ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Drive toolkit by Katherine McEldoon connecting cognitive science principles to practical learning design decisions. Bridges learning science theory and LE design practice.

programs.name: Bror Saxberg — MIT Festival of Learning Keynote 2023 (YouTube)
programs.summary: Keynote address by Bror Saxberg at the 2023 MIT Festival of Learning. Covers the vision of learning engineering, the gap between learning science and practice, and the role of data-driven design.

programs.name: QIP Learning Engineering Video Series (YouTube playlist)
programs.summary: YouTube playlist produced by Quality Information Partners featuring practitioners and researchers discussing learning engineering principles, case studies, and field development.

programs.name: Invitation to Learning Engineering Webinar Series (ICICLE, YouTube)
programs.summary: YouTube series of ICICLE-hosted webinars introducing the learning engineering field to new practitioners, covering foundational concepts, tools, and community resources.

programs.name: IEEE Learning Engineering Podcast
programs.summary: Official IEEE ICICLE podcast series featuring conversations with learning engineering researchers, practitioners, and field leaders. Available on PodServe and Spotify.

programs.name: Silver Lining for Learning — Art & Science of Learning Engineering (podcast, 2023)
programs.summary: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast exploring learning engineering as both an art and a science — balancing rigorous measurement with creative instructional design.

programs.name: Silver Lining for Learning — Human-Centered Methods in LE (podcast, 2023)
programs.summary: Episode of the Silver Lining for Learning podcast focused on human-centered design methods applied within the learning engineering process.

programs.name: Baker, Boser & Snow (2022) — Learning Engineering: A View on Where the Field Is At
programs.summary: Open-access article surveying the state of the learning engineering field as of 2022. Reviews definitions, practice norms, evidence standards, and key open problems.

programs.name: Craig et al. (2023) — Learning Engineering Perspectives for Educational Systems
programs.summary: Multi-author article framing how learning engineering perspectives can transform educational systems at scale. Addresses policy, infrastructure, and community dimensions.

programs.name: Lee (2023) — Learning Sciences & Learning Engineering: Natural or Artificial Distinction?
programs.summary: Scholarly article examining the relationship between learning sciences and learning engineering. Argues for integration rather than separation, tracing epistemological differences and convergences.

programs.name: Kolodner (2023) — Learning Engineering: What It Is, Why I'm Involved
programs.summary: Personal essay and scholarly reflection by Janet Kolodner on the nature of learning engineering, its roots in learning sciences, and her reasons for engagement with the field.

programs.name: 7 Things You Should Know About Learning Engineering (EDUCAUSE, 2018)
programs.summary: Accessible EDUCAUSE brief introducing learning engineering to higher-education technologists and administrators. Covers definition, typical activities, prospects, and challenges.

programs.name: High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering (Baker & Boser, 2021)
programs.summary: Report by Ryan Baker and Beth Boser identifying the highest-leverage opportunities for learning engineering investment. Covers evidence gaps, scale barriers, and field infrastructure needs.

programs.name: OpenSimon Community (CMU Simon Initiative)
programs.summary: Open-access community and toolkit collection from CMU's Simon Initiative. Hosts LearnSphere DataShop, OLI course materials, and research infrastructure for learning engineering at scale.

programs.name: MIT LEAP Group — Learning Engineering and Practice
programs.summary: MIT research group applying learning engineering principles to university-level STEM education. Produces open courseware, edX materials, and research on evidence-based pedagogy at scale.

programs.name: Playful Journey Lab / MITili (MIT)
programs.summary: MIT lab focused on game-based and playful learning engineering. MITili (MIT Integrated Learning Initiative) applies data-driven, iterative design to immersive and game-based educational experiences.

programs.name: The Learning Agency — EdSurge LE Series
programs.summary: Collection of practitioner-oriented articles from The Learning Agency published in EdSurge. Covers LE field development, practitioner perspectives, and connections to education reform.

programs.name: AECT Learning Engagement Activated Podcast — Jim Goodell Episode (2022)
programs.summary: Podcast episode featuring Jim Goodell discussing learning engineering principles, the ICICLE community, and the future of evidence-based learning design in professional development contexts.

programs.name: Goodell (2019) — Are You Doing Learning Engineering or Instructional Design?
programs.summary: Practitioner article by Jim Goodell drawing the distinction between instructional design and learning engineering. Emphasizes data use, iteration, and empirical grounding as key differentiators.

programs.name: SchoolSims — LE-Based Teacher Preparation Simulator (2025)
programs.summary: Simulation platform for teacher preparation applying learning engineering principles, cognitive task analysis, and naturalistic decision-making. Uses scenario-based assessment and data-driven iteration.

programs.name: Learning Engineering at a Glance (Schatz, Goodell & Kessler, Army Press, 2023)
programs.summary: Concise overview of learning engineering for military and defense training contexts, published by US Army Press. Bridges LE field concepts with high-consequence domain workforce applications.

programs.name: State of XR & Immersive Learning Report 2021 (iLRN / ICICLE)
programs.summary: Annual state-of-the-field report on extended reality (XR/VR/AR) in learning contexts, co-produced with ICICLE. Covers research landscape, practitioner adoption, and evidence base for immersive learning.

programs.name: Data Use Throughout the Learning Engineering Process (ICICLE graphic)
programs.summary: Infographic and supporting documentation mapping how different types of data — formative, summative, behavioral, survey — are collected and used at each stage of the LE process.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Adjacent Academic Programs Directory (ICICLE)
programs.summary: Google Doc directory of academic programs adjacent to or incorporating learning engineering, including LE-specific degrees, learning sciences programs, educational technology programs, and HCI programs.

programs.name: Learning Engineering is a Team Sport (ICICLE presentation, 2024)
programs.summary: ICICLE presentation emphasizing the collaborative, multidisciplinary nature of learning engineering teams. Addresses how different domain experts contribute to LE projects and how to build effective teams.

programs.name: Teaming Up to Improve Medical & Healthcare Education (Kurzweil & Marcellas, 2020)
programs.summary: Case study and practitioner article applying learning engineering team approaches to medical and healthcare professional education. Demonstrates LE in a high-stakes, complex domain.

programs.name: Build a Learning Data Dream Team (Torrance, Lin & Goodell, ATD, 2024)
programs.summary: Practitioner article on assembling multidisciplinary teams to leverage learning data effectively. Covers roles (data analyst, learning engineer, instructional designer) and collaborative workflows.

programs.name: IDEO Design Thinking Process (ideou.com)
programs.summary: IDEO's publicly available overview of the human-centered design thinking process. Referenced in LE as a precursor and complement to the iterative LE process, particularly for empathy and problem framing stages.

programs.name: Van Campenhout et al. (2023) — Student-Centered Educational Data Science Through Learning Engineering
programs.summary: Research article integrating educational data science methods within a learning engineering framework. Demonstrates how student-centered analysis and iterative design reinforce each other in practice.

programs.name: Herb Simon (1967) — The Job of a College President
programs.summary: Archival essay by Herbert A. Simon — foundational thinker behind cognitive science and bounded rationality — on university leadership and the role of evidence in educational decision-making. Cited as a historical intellectual ancestor of learning engineering.

programs.name: IEEE 1484.1-2003: Learning Technology Systems Architecture (LTSA)
programs.summary: Defines the high-level system components and communication protocols required for interoperable learning technology platforms. Foundational systems architecture standard for the field.

programs.name: IEEE 9274 Family (xAPI / Experience API)
programs.summary: Data instrumentation standard governing how learning experiences are tracked, stored in Learning Record Stores (LRS), and analyzed across distributed platforms. Includes JSON-LD application profiles, cybersecurity protocols, and vocabulary registries.

programs.name: IEEE P2247.1 & P2247.2: Adaptive Instructional Systems (AIS)
programs.summary: Classifies components, parameters, and interoperability protocols for adaptive instructional systems — engineering blueprints for building systems that dynamically adjust instruction based on real-time learner state.

programs.name: IEEE 1484.20.2: Recommended Practice for Defining Competencies
programs.summary: Rigorous data schema for defining human competencies. Supports Integrated Learner Records (ILR) spanning K-12, higher education, workforce, and military environments.

programs.name: IEEE 1484.12.1-2002 & P2881: Learning Object Metadata (LOM)
programs.summary: Essential standards for cataloging, discovery, and semantic structuring of digital learning resources across repositories. P2881 is the modernized successor incorporating linked data and accessibility requirements.

programs.name: Open Context Exchange (OCX)
programs.summary: OER schema: specification and metadata ontology for publishing Open Educational Resources on the web. Unifies content to a standard vocabulary for discoverability and automated remixing.

programs.name: IEEE ICICLE SIGs & MIGs
programs.summary: Official listing of IEEE ICICLE Special Interest Groups (SIGs) and Market Interest Groups (MIGs): chairs, meeting cadence, and how to subscribe (including ICICLE-CCC and other SIG lists via ListServ@ieee.org per IEEE instructions). Open participation; primary roster of active community leaders beyond the main conference.

programs.name: Jodi Lis
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Competencies, Credentials and Curriculum SIG and the Globalisation/Localisation SIG within IEEE ICICLE. Entry point for credentialing and curriculum alignment discussions in the LE community.

programs.name: Bedriye Akson
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Competencies, Credentials and Curriculum SIG (IEEE ICICLE), focused on competencies for learning engineering projects.

programs.name: Aaron Kessler
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Design for Learning SIG: human-centered design within the learning engineering process across implementation levels.

programs.name: Amy Parent
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Design for Learning SIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Adesola Ogundimu
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Globalisation/Localisation SIG: adapting the learning engineering process in local and international contexts.

programs.name: Mei-Mei Li
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG: mentorship and career development for students and recent graduates in learning engineering and adjacent fields.

programs.name: Abby Bregman
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Li (Lee) Liang
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Students and Grads SIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Erin Czerwinski
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Tools SIG (IEEE page lists as chair line; shared leadership with Baptiste Moreau-Pernet): tools for learning engineering practice, data collection, and analysis.

programs.name: Baptiste Moreau-Pernet
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Tools SIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Emily Marasco
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Higher Education MIG alongside Rob Nyland (IEEE page uses a “Chair” line for the MIG): learning engineering in higher education, instructional design, and research-informed improvement.

programs.name: Rob Nyland
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Higher Education MIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Eric Ultes
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Government / Military MIG: learning interventions in government and military contexts.

programs.name: Jason Virtue
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Government / Military MIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: Zarka Ali
programs.summary: Co-chair of the pK-12 MIG: learning engineering in preK–12 contexts, instructional design, and analytics-informed improvement.

programs.name: John Ellis
programs.summary: Co-chair of the pK-12 MIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: John Costa
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Workforce MIG: corporate learning and development leaders discussing learning engineering in workplace settings.

programs.name: DJ Jaeger
programs.summary: Co-chair of the Workforce MIG (IEEE ICICLE).

programs.name: METALS / MS in Learning Engineering (Carnegie Mellon)
programs.summary: Graduate program (long known as METALS) training learning engineers in design, learning science, analytics, and intelligent learning systems; capstone with external clients. Public materials may use MS in Learning Engineering naming.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Fellowship (CMU OLI)
programs.summary: Nine-week intensive for educators and designers to apply learning science and data-informed methods to real educational products and contexts; part of OLI professional learning.

programs.name: MIT Learning Engineering and Practice (LEAP) Group
programs.summary: Research group applying systems and design methods to STEM and workforce learning; emphasizes AI, simulation, XR, haptics, MOOCs, and curriculum at scale.

programs.name: LearnLab & LearnLab professional programs (CMU / network)
programs.summary: Research network and professional offerings (e.g., data analysis and modeling workshops, summer school) connecting cognitive science, LearnSphere/DataShop, and course improvement for formal and informal educators.

programs.name: Siemens Pathway to Learning Engineering
programs.summary: Cloud-based, project-based STEM curriculum and educator PD introducing engineering design and industry tools for secondary classrooms; complements university LE pathways.

programs.name: ASU Learning Engineering Institute & Graduate Certificate
programs.summary: Graduate certificate and research network fusing human systems engineering, design, and evidence to improve educational systems; connects students with Learning Engineering Research Network partners.

programs.name: Indiana University Luddy — ISE & human-centered computing
programs.summary: Large informatics and engineering school where Intelligent Systems Engineering, human-centered computing, and data-driven design converge—common feeder disciplines for learning engineering careers even where the phrase is not a standalone degree title.

programs.name: John Liu
programs.summary: Principal investigator for the MIT Learning Engineering and Practice (LEAP) group; leads work on digital learning, XR/simulation, and STEM workforce education at scale.

programs.name: Norman Bier
programs.summary: Director of OLI; bridges learning science, product, and partnerships for evidence-based online courses and professional programs including learning engineering training.

programs.name: Scotty Craig
programs.summary: Professor and leader in ASU Learning Engineering Institute initiatives; graduate program leadership for the learning engineering graduate certificate and related human-systems research.

programs.name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics (UPenn GSE)
programs.summary: GSE hub for learning analytics and AI in education research, including work that explicitly intersects learning engineering, educational data mining, and design of data-informed learning systems. Related graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Learning Analytics and Artificial Intelligence.

programs.name: Stanford GSE — M.S. Learning Design and Technology (LDT)
programs.summary: Residential master’s focused on designing and evaluating technology-enhanced learning environments using learning sciences, design methods, and empirical study—one of the clearest Stanford pathways aligned with learning engineering practice (even when the degree title says LDT, not LE).

programs.name: LIVE Learning Innovation Incubator (Vanderbilt Peabody)
programs.summary: Peabody incubator supporting interdisciplinary learning innovation; Peabody teams participate in national AI-for-learning efforts (e.g., AI-ALOE partner work such as intelligent text and workplace learning tools).

programs.name: National AI Institute for Adult Learning and Online Education (AI-ALOE)
programs.summary: NSF- and Accenture-funded national institute headquartered at Georgia Tech, developing AI theories and systems for adult online learning, intelligent tutors, and workforce upskilling in partnership with universities and industry.

programs.name: University of Washington — Learning sciences & design (CoE, HCDE, iSchool)
programs.summary: Graduate hub in Learning Sciences & Human Development plus related units (e.g., Learning, Epistemology, and Design Lab in HCDE; learning sciences at the Information School) for research on learning, design, and technology in formal and informal settings—UW’s closest cluster to learning engineering even without a single LE degree name.

programs.name: Purdue School of Engineering Education (ENE)
programs.summary: First-in-the-nation school of engineering education; graduate offerings include the online M.S. in Engineering Education, Ph.D. in engineering education research, and the stackable Teaching and Learning in Engineering graduate certificate—explicit “learning engineering” language appears in certificate and course titles.

programs.name: Herbert A. Simon
programs.summary: Nobel laureate (1978) who coined the term 'learning engineer' in the 1967 Educational Record essay 'The Job of a College President.' Argued that teaching effectiveness is a domain of expertise separate from subject-matter knowledge, requiring its own scientific foundation in cognitive science. Co-developed ACT cognitive theory with John Anderson. His work on bounded rationality, human problem solving, and cognitive simulation laid the intellectual foundation for intelligent tutoring systems. The Simon Initiative at CMU is named in his honor.

programs.name: John R. Anderson
programs.summary: Cognitive scientist and architect of ACT-R (Adaptive Control of Thought–Rational), the dominant cognitive architecture for modeling skill learning. Created the LISP Tutor (1983) — the first empirically validated intelligent tutoring system — and co-developed the Cognitive Tutor for algebra, deployed in 2,600+ schools to over half a million students. Demonstrated that ITS-based tutoring produces learning gains equivalent to one-to-one human tutoring (the '2-sigma effect'), providing the empirical foundation for personalized adaptive learning.

programs.name: Allen Newell
programs.summary: Pioneer of artificial intelligence and cognitive science. Co-developed SOAR — a unified theory of cognition modeling problem solving and learning through chunking — with Laird and Rosenbloom. With Herbert Simon, created the Logic Theorist (1956) and General Problem Solver (1957), the first AI programs, and established the physical symbol system hypothesis. SOAR's chunking mechanism provides a computational model of procedural skill acquisition that directly informs ITS problem-sequence design and mastery criteria.

programs.name: John Sweller
programs.summary: Educational psychologist who formalized Cognitive Load Theory (CLT), distinguishing intrinsic, extraneous, and germane cognitive load. Established that working memory constraints are a hard design constraint for instruction. CLT-derived principles — the worked examples effect, split-attention effect, and redundancy effect — are among the most replicated findings in educational psychology and are now standard learning engineering design rules. The 1988 founding paper has 4,000+ citations; the CLT literature it seeded spans hundreds of empirical studies.

programs.name: Ann L. Brown
programs.summary: Educational psychologist and pioneer of design-based research (DBR) and collaborative learning. 'Design Experiments: Theoretical and Methodological Challenges' (Journal of the Learning Sciences, 1992) is the most-cited paper in the learning sciences (2,800+ citations), establishing DBR as the canonical methodology for testing engineered learning interventions in authentic classrooms. Co-created Fostering Communities of Learners. Her methodological framework defines the epistemological standards learning engineering uses when validating systems in messy real-world contexts.

programs.name: Marlene Scardamalia
programs.summary: Pioneer of knowledge-building communities and computer-supported collaborative learning (CSCL). With Carl Bereiter, co-developed Knowledge Forum (formerly CSILE), the first networked collaborative knowledge-building environment. 'Computer Support for Knowledge-Building Communities' (Journal of the Learning Sciences, 1994) has 1,800+ citations and established the socio-cognitive framework for collaborative digital learning platforms — foundational for modern LMS design, MOOCs, and collaborative LE systems. Distinguished by emphasis on collective knowledge construction over individual optimization.

programs.name: Chris Dede
programs.summary: Pioneer of immersive learning environments and hybrid education. 'Immersive interfaces for engagement and learning' (Science, 2009, 2,000+ citations) established engineering parameters for using VR/AR in STEM education. Co-edited Learning Engineering for Online Education (Routledge, 2019) — the field's first major book — framing online architectures as enabling continuous telemetry that makes LE at scale legible. Post-pandemic work positioned hybrid learning as an optimal long-term state rather than an emergency measure.

programs.name: Neil Heffernan
programs.summary: Director of Learning Sciences and Technologies at WPI and creator of ASSISTments, a free intelligent tutoring platform serving 500,000+ students and 20,000+ teachers. Built E-TRIALS within ASSISTments — one of the first platforms enabling randomized controlled experiments at scale in live classrooms. Pioneered teacher-authored hint sequences and demonstrated that formative assessment integrated with intelligent hints produces significant learning gains. Research spans educational data mining, learning analytics, and optimization of student learning pathways.

programs.name: Karen Willcox
programs.summary: Led MIT's Online Education Policy Initiative (OEPI), producing the 2016 report 'Online Education: A Catalyst for Higher Education Reforms' that re-introduced 'learning engineer' to higher education discourse at the moment MOOCs reached 58M+ students. The report recommended universities create dedicated LE roles and directly catalyzed institutional adoption of LE language. Connected learning engineering to computational science methodologies from aerospace engineering, exemplifying the cross-disciplinary engineering approach the field advocates.

programs.name: Albert T. Corbett
programs.summary: Co-developer of Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) with John Anderson — the probabilistic model for tracking per-student, per-skill mastery that remains the most widely deployed student model in production ITS worldwide. Co-designed the ACT Programming Tutor and Algebra Cognitive Tutor. Systematic empirical work on learning curves, error analysis, and mastery criteria established the validation standards for ITS student models. 'Knowledge Tracing: Modeling the Acquisition of Procedural Knowledge' (1994) has 2,500+ citations.

programs.name: The Job of a College President
programs.summary: The founding document of learning engineering. Published in 1967 by Herbert Simon in the Educational Record, this essay coined the term 'learning engineer' and argued that teaching effectiveness is a domain of expertise distinct from subject-matter knowledge — requiring its own scientific grounding in cognitive science. Simon proposed that universities employ dedicated learning engineers to collaborate with faculty on evidence-based course design, translating cognitive science into instruction. The philosophical foundation for the field.

programs.name: Online Education: A Catalyst for Higher Education Reforms
programs.summary: 2016 report by Karen Willcox, Sanjay Sarma, and Philip Lippel that re-introduced 'learning engineer' to higher education discourse at the moment MOOCs reached 58M+ global students. Synthesized advances in cognitive science, neuroscience, education research, and edtech; recommended universities create dedicated learning engineering roles. Widely cited as the document that triggered the modern LE movement in higher education institutions and re-legitimized the Simon 1967 vision in the digital era.

programs.name: High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering
programs.summary: 2021 report by Ryan Baker, Ulrich Boser, and Allison Shelley synthesizing a 2020 convening of 100+ academics, policymakers, and practitioners. Defines ten strategic priorities across three domains: Better LE Infrastructure (shared R&D architectures, A/B testing at scale), Supporting Human Processes (teacher dashboards, predictive advising), and Better Learning Technologies (algorithmic equity, complex-skills measurement). The field's most comprehensive contemporary roadmap and the primary policy document cited when framing LE's current agenda.

programs.name: Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Teaching and Learning: Insights and Recommendations
programs.summary: 2023 federal policy document addressing AI in education with explicit civil-rights framing. Documented algorithmic bias in e-proctoring systems — facial recognition failures for darker-skinned, transgender, and neurodivergent students; misidentification of neurodivergent behaviors as academic dishonesty. Mandated algorithmic fairness and data representation requirements. Directly relevant to learning engineers designing or procuring AI-driven assessment and surveillance systems. Sets the ethical and legal floor for LE practice in the US.

programs.name: 7 Things You Should Know About Learning Engineering
programs.summary: 2018 practitioner-oriented brief in the canonical EDUCAUSE '7 Things' series, which introduced LE to a broad institutional technology audience including CIOs, provosts, and instructional technology directors. Accelerated LE vocabulary adoption in non-research higher education contexts. Widely cited as the accessible entry point for administrators and practitioners encountering the field for the first time.

programs.name: Learning Engineering Adoption Maturity Model (LEAMM)
programs.summary: Organizational capability matrix (2024/2025) allowing enterprises to assess and advance their LE practice across human-centered design, learning science integration, and data-driven iteration dimensions. Provides a structured pathway from ad-hoc instructional design toward mature learning engineering practice. The field's primary organizational self-assessment tool, developed by the ICICLE community including Goodell, Craig, Blake-Plock, and colleagues.

programs.name: OpenSimon Toolkit (CMU Simon Initiative)
programs.summary: The flagship open infrastructure for learning engineering research globally. Comprises DataShop (world's largest open educational interaction data repository), LearnSphere (open learning analytics infrastructure), CTAT (cognitive tutor authoring tools), OLI (Open Learning Initiative courseware), DiscourseDB (NLP for learning interactions), LightSide (automated text coding), and Bazaar (intelligent collaborative chatbot). Enables researchers worldwide to build, test, and study learning engineering systems without proprietary infrastructure.

programs.name: The Simon Initiative — Carnegie Mellon University
programs.summary: Cross-disciplinary learning engineering ecosystem at CMU named for Herbert Simon. Encompasses LearnLab (in-vivo research lab), OLI (courseware platform), DataShop/LearnSphere (world's largest educational log data), METALS master's program, and the OpenSimon Toolkit. Hundreds of faculty; the most integrated LE research-to-practice ecosystem globally. Produces both the theoretical advances and the open infrastructure that defines modern learning engineering.

programs.name: Penn Center for Learning Analytics
programs.summary: Research center directing educational data mining and learning engineering policy work, led by Ryan Baker. Produced 'High-Leverage Opportunities for Learning Engineering' (2021) and several landmark EDM papers. Baker is editor of the Journal of Educational Data Mining and a primary organizer of the EDM conference. Major hub connecting academic EDM research to policy influence and practitioner adoption.

programs.name: Journal of the Learning Sciences
programs.summary: Founded 1991; the multidisciplinary anchor journal for theoretical models, epistemology, and design-based research in the learning sciences. Home of Ann Brown's 'Design Experiments' (1992, 2,800+ citations) and Scardamalia & Bereiter's knowledge-building paper (1994, 1,800+ citations). Publishes the socio-cognitive and humanistic tradition within LE — essential for grounding algorithmic approaches in rich theories of how people learn in context.

programs.name: Journal of Educational Data Mining
programs.summary: Open-access journal dedicated to algorithms and methodologies for analyzing educational data: knowledge tracing, automated feature engineering, predictive student modeling, and behavioral analysis. Baker & Yacef's founding codification of EDM (2009) published here. Premier venue for computational LE methods. Diamond open access — no fees for authors or readers.

programs.name: IEEE Transactions on Learning Technologies
programs.summary: Covers technical design and architecture of learning systems: adaptive algorithms, NLP conversational agents, multimodal telemetry (eye tracking, EEG, galvanic skin response), and large-scale platform engineering. Requires substantive technical innovation in computer science alongside pedagogical validity. Key venue for AI-in-education engineering papers and Transformer-based conversational agent research.

programs.name: Journal of Learning Analytics
programs.summary: Official publication of SoLAR. Covers socio-technical implications of educational data: dashboard design, algorithmic ethics, data privacy, human-in-the-loop architectures, and equity in analytics deployment. Complements JEDM's algorithmic focus with practice implications and ethical framing — the two journals represent the LA/EDM distinction that should be preserved in the corpus taxonomy.

programs.name: International Journal of Artificial Intelligence in Education
programs.summary: Premier venue for ITS, generative AI in education, deep cognitive modeling, and evaluation of AI vs. human tutoring. Covers algorithmic accuracy evaluations, human factors studies, and pedagogical agent design. Craig & Schroeder (2018) design principles for virtual humans published here. The home journal for rigorous AIED research between conference cycles.

programs.name: Journal of Learning Engineering
programs.summary: The only journal specifically dedicated to learning engineering as a named field. Diamond Open Access — no fees for authors or readers. Community-driven by ICICLE; provides the 'Learning Engineering Primer' to align complex multidisciplinary terminology among authors. Because the field is young and sparsely indexed, JoLE papers often do not appear in citation-network-based discovery — venue search is required. Added as a venue query in icicle_adjacent_conference_queries.json.

programs.name: International Conference on Intelligent Tutoring Systems
programs.summary: The oldest dedicated ITS/LE conference, founded 1988 in Montreal. Biennial; proceedings published by Springer LNCS. Premier venue for cognitive models, student modeling, adaptive instruction, and feedback algorithm research. ITS2026 in Cyprus (June 1–5, 2026). ITS and AIED now alternate years, making one or the other the annual flagship AI-in-education conference.

programs.name: HCII — Human-Computer Interaction Institute, Carnegie Mellon
programs.summary: Academic home of learning sciences and technologies at CMU. Houses Ken Koedinger, Al Corbett, and the PACT Center. Offers PhD and MS programs integrating HCI, learning sciences, and data science. Co-originator of learning engineering as a named field; home of DataShop, CTAT, and the Cognitive Tutor research lineage. One of the two most important institutional anchors for LE research globally (alongside the Simon Initiative).
